first_order.why: measured error is consistent with the device's published physics
first_order.consilience: two independent oracles agree: the ideal re-derivation AND IBM's daily-benchmarked calibration both bound this measurement — corroboration on a physical result
first_order.note: GATE = ideal (re-derived). ATTEST = hardware measurement (the world). The calibration noise model gates whether the measurement is physically admissible. First-order model: order-of-magnitude consistency, not an exact fidelity claim.

calibrated.why: noise signature consistent with the device's calibrated physics
calibrated.resolution_note: with 1024 shots, 'too clean' on this device is NOT statistically resolvable — needs more shots
calibrated.method: noise-signature TVD vs Aer NoiseModel.from_backend, shot-noise-aware margin
calibrated.consilience: ideal re-derivation AND IBM's full calibrated noise model both bound this measurement
